ebellum HUMAN ANATOMY LAB MANUAL 59

STATION FIVE: BRAIN DISSECTION WITHOUT MENINGES • Orientate the brain such that the posterior aspect containing the brain stem is facing you. Obtain the scalpel from your kit and place it on the anterior (farthest from you) portion of the longitudinal fissure. Using a scalpel, firmly press down on the brain while simultaneously bringing the scalpel carefully towards you; this makes a clean incision down the sagittal plane of the brain. (DO NOT make sawing motions with the scalpel.) Continue to make incisions until you have separated the brain into its two hemispheres. • Obtain pins from the table and place them into as many structures as you can identify. Your lab TA will come around and ask you to identify the pins you have placed. *If you are the last
